pi_items.source: Code analysis: confirmed in setup_task.sh line 16 (rm -f /home/ga/graph_solved.png) and export_result.sh line 10 (EXPECTED_PATH="/home/ga/graph_solved.png")

Web search and official GCompris documentation confirm graph_coloring is a real activity in GCompris created by Akshat Tandon during Season of KDE (SoK). Activity goal: color nodes so no two adjacent nodes share the same color.

Web search of official GCompris screenshots page indicates categories are: All, Computer, Discovery, Sciences, Fun, Mathematics, Puzzle, Reading, Strategy. Graph Coloring categorization retrieved from gcompris.net/screenshots-en.html showed 'Discovery' and 'Logic', but 'Logic' does not appear in the official category list. No evidence found for 'Logic/Strategy' as a single combined category name.

privileged_info_summary: The Graph Coloring activity is a verified real activity in GCompris created by Akshat Tandon during Season of KDE. The activity's goal is to color graph nodes so that no two adjacent nodes share the same color, helping develop strategy sense, color distinction, and position memorization. The expected screenshot path /home/ga/graph_solved.png is confirmed in the task scripts. However, the category 'Logic/Strategy' does not match official GCompris category names, which are: All, Computer, Discovery, Sciences, Fun, Mathematics, Puzzle, Reading, and Strategy.
